commit.message:
feat(control-plane): implement artifact and review packet retrieval endpoints

Add REST endpoints for retrieving task artifacts, review history,
review cycle packets, parsed packet content, and merge details.

Endpoints:
- GET /tasks/:taskId/artifacts - artifact tree organized by type
- GET /tasks/:taskId/packets/:packetId - parsed JSON packet content
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews - review cycle history with decisions
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews/:cycleId/packets - specialist packets
- GET /tasks/:taskId/merge - merge queue item + validation runs

New files:
- review/artifacts.controller.ts + artifacts.service.ts
- review/reviews.controller.ts + reviews.service.ts
- merge/merge-details.controller.ts + merge-details.service.ts
- 6 test files with 31 new tests (3,268 total passing)

Closes T084.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix cloud_agent merge backend: comment on PR instead of creating separate issue (#597)

* Initial plan

* fix: cloud_agent merge backend now comments on PR instead of creating new issue

The cloud agent backend for merge conflict resolution was creating a new
GitHub issue and assigning the Copilot coding agent to it. However, the
agent works on its own branch and cannot perform a proper git merge across
branches, resulting in PRs with 0 actual changes.

This changes the cloud_agent backend to post a @copilot comment directly
on the conflicting PR, asking the agent to reapply the changes on top of
the latest base branch. This approach works because the agent operates
within the PR context rather than on a separate branch.

Changes:
- core/src/state.rs: Add `comment_only` field to PendingMergeIssue
- cli/src/merge.rs: resolve_via_cloud_agent now uses comment_on_pr
- cli/src/merge.rs: promote_pending_merge_issues handles comment-only
  entries by checking PR conflict status
- Updated and added tests for the new behavior

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix unwrap_or for mergeable check, improve docs

* Initial plan

* feat: add pre-flight in-cluster API connectivity check before GitOps installation

Add WaitForInClusterAPIConnectivity in pkg/k8s/readiness/connectivity.go
that creates a short-lived busybox pod to verify TCP connectivity to the
API server ClusterIP from within the cluster. This catches eBPF dataplane
race conditions where Cilium DaemonSet pods report Ready but pod-to-service
routing is not yet fully programmed.

Integrate the check into waitForClusterStability in pkg/cli/setup/post_cni.go
as a third step after API server stability and DaemonSet readiness checks.

* Initial plan

* Fix merge ralph: guard against duplicate work and fix draft PR advancement

Two bugs fixed:

1. Added guard in run_merge to check for existing pending issues or tracked
   PRs before creating new conflict resolution work. This prevents duplicate
   issues from being filed for the same merge conflict.

   is_pr_work_completed() when the agent is still assigned to the issue.
   Some agents (e.g. Copilot) remain assigned after finishing, so the
   two-tier check (session completion + title heuristic) is now consulted
   before deciding to skip. This mirrors the same pattern already used in
   check_agent_status.

commit.message:
Add missing GDDR telemetry (#2215)

### Issue
#2164 

### Description
This pull request introduces comprehensive support for GDDR telemetry.
It adds new data structures, APIs, and exposes telemetry tags for
capturing detailed per-module and aggregated GDDR health and error
information. The changes also update relevant enums, APIs, and bindings
to use GDDR terminology and structures throughout the codebase.

### List of the changes
**GDDR Telemetry Support:**

* Introduced a new `gddr_telemetry.hpp` header defining `BlackholeGddr`,
`GddrModuleTelemetry`, and `GddrTelemetry` structures for detailed
per-module and aggregated GDDR monitoring.
* Added functions to `FirmwareInfoProvider` and its Wormhole
implementation for retrieving per-module and aggregated GDDR telemetry,
GDDR speed, and maximum temperature, using new telemetry tags.
* Returning nullopt for SMBUS telemetry as the old and new telemetry
don't match well (and will not be needed).

**Telemetry Tag and API Updates:**

* Replaced all references to DDR with GDDR in telemetry tags and related
enums, including new tags for per-module temperatures and error counts.
* Updated all code, including firmware info providers and telemetry
readers, to use GDDR tags and structures for reading and interpreting
telemetry data.

**Python Bindings:**

* Updated nanobind Python bindings to expose new GDDR telemetry tags and
structures, and to support access to GDDR telemetry data from Python.

**Internal Refactoring and Maintenance:**

* Added necessary includes and updated CMake and source files to ensure
new telemetry structures and tags are available throughout the device
codebase.


### Testing
Manual and CI

commit.message:
syscall: unconditionally route to VFS in standalone mode

In standalone mode the VFS is the only filesystem — there is no linuxd
to forward requests to. The previous code used is_vfs_path() (which
calls fat32_backend::exists()) to decide whether to route to VFS. This
caused two problems:

1. open/openat: Files being created (O_CREAT) or paths that don't exist
   yet were not recognized by is_vfs_path(), so the call fell through to
   either an IPC send to the non-existent linuxd (hanging forever) or an
   unhelpful 'not available in standalone mode' error.

2. stat/fstatat: Any stat on a non-existent path returned 'not available
   in standalone mode' instead of the expected ENOENT, breaking Python's
   import machinery which probes for .so/.pyc/.py files by stat'ing each
   candidate.

Fix all four syscall wrappers to unconditionally route to VFS when both
'standalone' and 'memfs' features are enabled. The VFS itself returns
proper error codes (e.g., ENOENT for missing files), which is the
correct POSIX behavior.

Also narrow the standalone-without-memfs fallback error in openat and
fstatat to only trigger when memfs is truly absent, avoiding unreachable
code after the unconditional VFS return.

leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,

  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries

  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

commit.message:
Redirect unscoped WP URLs back to scoped WP URLs in service worker (#3370)

## Summary
- When the service worker intercepts a request from a scoped page
(identified via the Referer header) but the request URL itself is
unscoped, redirect it to the scoped version of the URL instead of
directly handling it as a PHP request. This prevents scope loss when
navigating around a scoped WordPress instance.
- Skip intercepting cross-origin requests initiated from scoped pages,
letting them pass through to the network instead.

## Context

Previously, when a scoped WordPress page linked to an unscoped URL (e.g.
a WordPress-generated link without the scope prefix), the service worker
would directly handle it via `handleScopedRequest()`. This worked for
that single request, but the resulting page would be loaded at an
unscoped URL. Navigating from that unscoped page to another unscoped URL
resulted in losing the scope entirely, and the service worker would no
longer be able to match requests to the correct WordPress instance.

By redirecting to the scoped URL instead, the browser's address bar and
subsequent navigations all stay within the scope.

## Test plan
- [ ] Load a scoped WordPress Playground instance
- [ ] Navigate to a WordPress link that doesn't include the scope prefix
- [ ] Verify the browser redirects to the scoped version of the URL
- [ ] Verify subsequent navigation continues to work within the scope
- [ ] Verify cross-origin requests from scoped pages are not intercepted

commit.message:
refactor: improve error handling, types, and documentation

Preserve original error context in ScheduleFetchError, tighten formatter
map types with enum unions for compile-time exhaustiveness, and document
why LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams requires a transaction client.

Changes:
- Add cause option to ScheduleFetchError constructor and pass original error
- Add JSDoc to LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams explaining FOR UPDATE requirement
- Type SKILL_LEVEL_LABELS and LEARNING_STYLE_LABELS with enum Record types
- Narrow formatSkillLevel/formatLearningStyle parameters to enum unions
- Remove formatter tests for invalid string inputs (now compile errors)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): improve rate limit headers and error metadata

getRateLimitHeaders included Retry-After on every response, violating
RFC 6585 which reserves it for 429/503. RateLimitError only carried
retryAfter but the class supports limit/remaining/reset fields.

Changes:
- Remove Retry-After from getRateLimitHeaders (X-RateLimit-* only)
- Include limit, remaining, and reset in RateLimitError on rejection
- Update tests to reflect Retry-After removal from success headers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hooks): centralize stream cleanup in useRetryGeneration

Early returns on terminal SSE events (complete, error, cancelled) did
not cancel the reader or abort the controller, causing resource leaks.
Wrapping the reader loop in try/finally ensures cleanup on every exit
path.

Changes:
- Wrap reader loop and buffer-flush in inner try/finally block
- Call reader.cancel() and controller.abort() in finally clause

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): bind OAuth state token redemption to provider

The state redemption was not bound to the issuing provider, allowing a
token issued for one provider to be redeemed against another. consume()
now requires and verifies a provider parameter, preventing cross-provider
token replay attacks.

Changes:
- Add provider parameter to OAuthStateStore.consume interface
- Add eq(provider) to the WHERE clause in consume implementation
- Update validateOAuthStateToken to require and forward provider
- Pass google_calendar provider in Google OAuth callback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(logging): align correlation ID header with proxy

The proxy sets x-correlation-id on every request, but REQUEST_ID_HEADER
in request-context.ts was x-request-id. createRequestContext therefore
never found the correlation ID the proxy set, generating a fresh UUID
and breaking request correlation across the stack.

Changes:
- Change REQUEST_ID_HEADER from x-request-id to x-correlation-id
- attachRequestIdHeader automatically picks up the new value
